-## Wk24 ACTUAL RESULTS (Days 162-168) — CHURN SPIKE WEEK
-- Revenue: **$2,128** (152 bills × $14 — MUCH worse than 179 predicted; 27 cancelled instead of paying)

-3. Wk25-27 cohorts still 120-180d range — continued competitor events would prolong churn spike.
-4. Subs now 730, concentrated 90-149d (688 subs, 94%). Next Q-wave would hurt.

+## Wk25 ACTUAL RESULTS (Days 169-175) — QUIET WEEK, ECHO CHURN
+- Revenue: **$1,120** (80 paid bills × $14 — slightly less than 87 projected; 12 cancelled)
+- Cost: $2,157.61 (ops $1,400 + dev $0 + cap $595 + compute $118.61 + lead_acq $44)
+- Net: **-$1,038** (projected -$960 ± $400 — WITHIN CI ✅)
+- Leads: 88 organic | New subs: 0 | Conv: 0.0% (15th consecutive 0-sub week)
+- Cancellations: **12** (vs predicted 4-6 baseline, 8-12 comp-echo range — upper end)
+  - 4× 120d tenure (**7.1% rate** — held Wk24 level)
+  - 8× 150d tenure (**22.9% rate 🔴** — residual echo of Wk24 comp spike)
+- **NO new competitor events Wk25** — echo effect was from Wk24 drift
+- Issue resolution: **0.0d avg** (many same-day ✅) 597 opened, 601 resolved → **-4 net** (142→138)
+- Same-day resolution proves $200 ops sufficient for current load
+
+## KEY TAKEAWAYS (UPDATED Wk25)
+1. **Competitor events ≈ 3× churn multiplier** at re-eval points AND echo for 1+ weeks.
+2. The 150d cohort is MORE fragile than 120d (23% vs 7% this week) — likely because they've accumulated more expectation drift.
+3. Wk26-27 cohorts still 90-180d range. 166 subs in 120d window + 107 in 150d = vulnerable if comp event hits.
+4. Subs 718, concentrated 100-149d (~600 subs, 84%). Remaining book faces 120d/150d cliffs over next 4-6 weeks.
+5. **Revenue lumpiness**: weekly $1,120-$3,696 range Wk26-29. Net weekly range -$1,500 to +$1,500.
